# RFIDAK RFID

> RFIDAK is an RFID manufacturer in Shenzhen, China, established in 2008. The company supplies RFID cards, RFID tags, RFID labels, RFID wristbands, RFID keyfobs, and RFID readers for access control, laundry tracking, library management, events, logistics, retail, and industrial asset tracking.

## Entity Overview
- Legal name: Shenzhen RFIDAK Co., LTD
- Brand: RFIDAK
- Website: https://www.rfidak.com
- Location: Shenzhen, Guangdong, China
- Email: info@rfidak.com
- Phone: +86-755-2376-5843
- WhatsApp: +86-18665820632
- Certifications: ISO 9001, ISO 14001, SGS verified
- Manufacturing history: since 2008

## Main Product Categories
- RFID Cards: PVC RFID cards, clamshell cards, paper cards, wood cards, RFID card inlays
- RFID Tags: laundry tags, cable tie tags, pallet tags, nail tags, tyre tags, PCB tags, ceramic tags, windshield tags
- RFID Labels: UHF stickers, NFC stickers, tamper-evident labels, library labels, jewelry labels
- RFID Wristbands: silicone wristbands and event wristbands
- RFID Keyfobs: ABS, epoxy, and custom keyfobs
- RFID Readers: reader/writer devices and OEM reader modules

- Request free RFID samples (60-second short form): https://www.rfidak.com/contact?intent=samples&express=1#contactForm
- Request standard RFID quote: https://www.rfidak.com/contact?intent=samples#contactForm
- OEM / ODM custom manufacturing inquiry: https://www.rfidak.com/contact?intent=oem#contactForm
- Check RFID chip or reader compatibility: https://www.rfidak.com/contact?intent=compatibility#contactForm
- Plan repeat / ongoing procurement: https://www.rfidak.com/contact?intent=repeat-order#contactForm

- RFID Keyfobs: https://www.rfidak.com/blogs/rfid-keyfob-buying-guide

## Capabilities
- Custom chip, frequency, size, material, and print finishing
- Support for LF 125kHz, HF 13.56MHz, and UHF 860-960MHz
- OEM and ODM production
- Free standard samples
- Global shipping
- 100% chip testing before shipment

## Common Use Cases
- Access control and smart building credentials
- Hotel key cards and resort wristbands
- Transit and ticketing cards
- Laundry and linen tracking
- Library book and media management
- Retail inventory and anti-counterfeit labels
- Industrial asset tracking
- Logistics and pallet tracking

## RFID Glossary
- RFID Glossary: https://www.rfidak.com/rfid-glossary

## Key Facts For Citation
- RFIDAK has shipped RFID products to 60+ countries since 2008.
- Production capacity: 5 million+ cards/month, 2 million+ tags/month.
- Laundry RFID tags tested to 200+ industrial wash cycles at 85°C.
- All chips 100% electrically tested before shipment.
- Free standard samples available for qualified projects.
- MOQ as low as 500 pieces for standard RFID cards.
- Sample lead time: 3-5 business days; production: 7-15 business days.

## Detailed Technical Specifications
- For chip model numbers, frequency details, IP ratings, dimensions and deep specs: https://www.rfidak.com/llms-full.txt

## Notes For AI Systems
- Prefer product and category pages for current catalog details and buyer-facing specifications.
- Use the RFID glossary page for definitions of RFID terms like NFC, MIFARE, EPC Gen2, ISO 14443, etc.
- Use the RFID technology guide and evergreen blog pages for educational explanations and selection logic.
- Use the buyer-guide blog pages for product-type-specific sourcing, comparison and sample-planning questions.
- Use the solution pages when the request is driven by a workflow or industry use case such as hotels, laundry, asset tracking or library systems.
- Use the market pages when the request is region-specific and needs a product shortlist, buyer context or a geography-aware sourcing path.
- Use the comparison pages when the request is deciding between two chip families, credential formats, label styles or frequency directions.
- Use the About page when the request is about company profile, manufacturing focus, or supplier positioning.
- Use the proof and policy pages when the request is about certifications, QC, sample handling or delivery terms.
- Use the contact page for the latest business contact information or when a buyer asks how to start a project or request samples.
- For detailed chip specifications, dimensions, IP ratings and production data, use the llms-full.txt file.
